title: Smashed Cucumber Salad
tagline: The viral crunchy, garlicky, spicy cucumber situation.
description: Chinese-style smashed cucumbers (pai huang gua) with garlic, chili oil, and rice vinegar. Smashing creates jagged edges that hold more sauce. Dangerously addictive.
ingredients: 1-2 Tbsp chili crisp or chili oil
steps: Place cucumbers on cutting board. Using the flat side of a knife or cleaver, smash each cucumber firmly until it cracks and splits. Cut into rough 1-inch pieces.
Toss cucumber pieces with salt in a colander. Let drain 10-15 minutes to draw out excess water. This keeps the salad from getting soggy.
While cucumbers drain, whisk together garlic, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, chili crisp, and sugar.
Pat cucumbers dry with paper towels. Transfer to serving bowl.
Pour dressing over cucumbers and toss well, making sure sauce gets into all the cracks and crevices.
Garnish with sesame seeds and cilantro. Serve immediately for maximum crunch.
notes: Smashing is better than cutting - the rough edges absorb more sauce.
Salting and draining is crucial. Skip this and you get a watery salad.
Best eaten within 30 minutes. The cucumbers will continue to release water.
